Sesuai dengan pasal 3 Anggaran Dasar In accordance with article 3 of the Company's Perusahaan, Perusahaan bergerak dalam bidang Articles of Association, the Company is engaged in usaha peternakan unggas, kegiatan rumah potong the business of poultry farming, poultry meat dan pengepakan daging unggas dan jasa penunjang slaughterhouse and packing activities and livestock peternakan. Saat ini, Perusahaan melakukan supporting services. Currently, the Company kegiatan antara lain budidaya ayam ras pedaging conducts activities including broiler and layer breed dan ayam ras petelur; pembibitan dan budidaya chicken cultivation; local chicken breeding and ayam lokal; kegiatan rumah potong ayam, serta jasa cultivation; chicken slaughterhouse activities, and penetasan telur. Perusahaan mulai beroperasi egg hatching services. The Company started its secara komersial pada akhir tahun 2007. commercial operations at the end of 2007. Perusahaan berdomisili di Yogyakarta dan kantor The Company is domiciled in Yogyakarta and the Perushaan berlokasi di Ruko Casa Grande No. 35 Company's office is located at Ruko Casa Grande Jalan Ring Road Utara, Maguwoharjo, Kecamatan No. 35 Jalan Ring Road Utara, Maguwoharjo, Depok Depok, Kabupaten Sleman. UMUM (Lanjutan) 1. GENERAL (Continued) b. Dewan Komisaris, Direksi dan Karyawan b. Board of Commissioners, Directors and Employees Susunan Dewan Komisaris dan Direksi Perusahaan The composition of the Company's Board of pada tanggal 30 September 2025 dan 31 Desember Commissioners and Directors as at September 2024 adalah sebagai berikut: 30,2025 and December 31, 2024 is as follows:

Shareholders Unlocking Midstream Upside and Rebalancing Asset Mix AYAM’s strategy is centered on asset optimization and upstream integration, particularly through direct Haji Singgih GPS importation to bridge the gap between upstream DOC availability and downstream commercial farm Januratmoko, : 76.5% capacity. With DOC Final Stock of ~45 mn birds vs. commercial broiler capacity of 795 mn birds, the SKH company has substantial room to unlock midstream upside. Operationally, the predominance of closed- Public : 21.0% house farming systems supports higher productivity, lower mortality, and more consistent live weights. Strategically, management is expanding exposure to breeding, slaughterhouse, and commercial layer Non-public <5% : 2.5% operations, aiming to reduce reliance on volatile broiler pricing and external DOC supply while improving margin stability through a more balanced and integrated asset mix. Earnings Recovery in Sight AYAM recorded revenue of IDR225.4 bn in 9M25 (-15.9% YoY), reflecting a sharp contraction in the commercial broiler segment (-32.1% YoY), partially offset by strong growth in the DOC segment (+75.1% YoY). With COGS declining more moderately (-7.2% YoY), EBITDA turned negative at IDR-14.2 bn, resulting in a net loss of IDR-16.7 bn in 9M25. We view this as largely cyclical, driven by broiler price weakness in 2Q–3Q25 and elevated feed costs. Looking ahead, a tighter supply environment, MBG-driven demand support, and ongoing asset mix rebalancing toward higher-margin DOC, layer, and slaughtering activities are expected to underpin a gradual and more sustainable earnings recovery, supporting improved medium-term profitability and shareholder value creation. Valuation: Non Rated AYAM’s share price has risen +147% over the past 3-months, broadly tracking the positive sentiment across the poultry sector, alongside gains in peers such as JPFA (+21%), MAIN (+68%), and WMUU (+71%). The recent share price movement appears to be driven primarily by sector-level re-rating, reflecting expectations of improved supply discipline, policy-driven demand support, and a gradual normalization in industry fundamentals, rather than a clear recovery in near-term earnings. Within this context, AYAM’s vertically integrated operations, scalable production assets, and ongoing asset mix rebalancing toward DOC, layer, and slaughtering activities place the stock within the broader poultry valuation range. At present, AYAM is trading at PBV of approximately 9.2x, which appears to reflect market expectations of medium-term earnings recovery.
